question: What was the total gross AFE estimate for the Courts Federal 1H well?
notes: AFE #140319, operator EnCana. Accept $2,773,800 exactly (±$1 rounding); reject the net estimate of $1,236,288.
agent_answer:
The total gross AFE estimate for the Courts Federal 1H well is **$2,773,800**.

This comes from AFE #140319, dated 2008-02-20, operated by EnCana Oil & Gas (USA) Inc. (Devon Energy Production Company, L.P. listed as a partner with 44.5702% working interest), for Drilling & Completion — source: (FP GRIFFIN/COURTS FEDERAL 1H/Financial/AFE/424393294700-COURTS_FEDERAL_1H-TAB_AFE-0002.pdf.pdf, page 1).

Breakdown of the gross estimate:
- Intangible – Drilling: $975,400
- Tangible – Drilling: $36,400
- Intangible – Completion: $1,390,000
- Tangible – Completion: $372,000
(Total: $2,773,800)

For reference, the corresponding total net estimate (at the 44.5702% WI) was $1,236,288 (same document, page 1). This is the only AFE document in the corpus sample for this well.

question: What was the maximum treating pressure recorded on the Wrangler N734H frac stage report?

expected_citations.key: FORMENTERA SOUTH TEXAS/WRANGLER-STX-UNIT N734H/Completions/Frac/WRANGLER-STX-UNIT N734H - Print Out - Stage 37.pdf

notes: Accept 10,998 psi (±10 psi). Do not confuse with fluid/proppant completion percentages on the same ticket.
agent_answer:
I checked all four Frac Report entries filed for **WRANGLER-STX-UNIT N734H** in the corpus:

| Document | Readable? | Contains a max treating pressure figure? |
|---|---|---|
| Stage 22 – Field Ticket | Yes (financial ticket, no pressure data) | No |
| Stage 66 – Treatment Plots | Yes (pressure/time charts only) | No extractable numeric max (chart axis only, no discrete reading) |
| Stage 63 – .csv | No — vendor/log binary format, not parseable | N/A |
| **Stage 37 – Print Out** | Yes, structured extraction | **Yes** |

The only frac stage report for this well with a clean, extracted maximum treating pressure value is **Stage 37**, which recorded a **maximum pressure of 10,998 psi** (FORMENTERA SOUTH TEXAS/WRANGLER-STX-UNIT N734H/Completions/Frac/WRANGLER-STX-UNIT N734H - Print Out - Stage 37.pdf, page 1). That same document also shows the frac ran from 23:58 to 2:28 with a 150‑minute pump time (same citation).

Note: Stage 66's plot pages do show treating-pressure curves (axis scaled to ~15,000–16,000 psi) but the parsed output is a chart image without a discrete peak-value readout, so I can't state a numeric max from it without risking a fabricated reading. If you need the true fleet-wide maximum across all N734H stages, the full archive's other stage reports/tickets (entry_type `Frac Report`) beyond this 500-file sample would need to be pulled.

question: Comparing their well tests, which well had the higher initial gas rate: the Boyd Billy 14H or the Deal 2H?
expected_answer: The Deal 2H (3,622 Mcf/day vs 2,364 Mcf/day for Boyd Billy 14H).

agent_answer:
Based on the well test reports for both wells, the **Deal 2H** had the higher initial gas rate.

- **Boyd, Billy 14H**: Initial Potential gas back-pressure test on 2010-12-29 recorded a gas rate of **2,364 Mcf/day** (FP GRIFFIN/BOYD, BILLY 14H/Production/Well Tests/422513404000-BOYD,_BILLY_14H-Completion_Reports-0001.pdf.PDF, p. 1).

These are the only well-test documents found in the corpus sample for each well, so the comparison is based on a single readable test per well. Deal 2H's initial gas rate (3,622 Mcf/day) is roughly 53% higher than Boyd Billy 14H's (2,364 Mcf/day).

question: Comparing per-stage frac costs, was the estimated stage cost higher on the Wrangler N731H stage or the Wildcat Hollow S512HF stage, and by roughly how much?
expected_answer: Wrangler N731H was higher: $39,304.60 vs $28,681.85 for Wildcat Hollow S512HF, a difference of about $10,623.

expected_citations.key: FORMENTERA SOUTH TEXAS/WRANGLER-STX-UNIT N731H/Completions/Frac/WRANGLER-STX-UNIT N731H - Ticket - Stage 8.pdf
WESTLAKE RESOURCES/WILDCAT HOLLOW-16-33-PGN S512HF/Completions/Frac/REPORT WILDCAT HOLLOW-16-33-PGN S512HF Stage 27.pdf

notes: Correct if it names Wrangler N731H as higher; accept the difference $10,623 (±$50) or the two values (±1%).
agent_answer:
## Answer

Comparing the only readable per‑stage frac cost tickets available for each well in this sample:

- **Wrangler N731H — Stage 8**: estimated stage cost **$39,304.60** (FORMENTERA SOUTH TEXAS/WRANGLER-STX-UNIT N731H/Completions/Frac/WRANGLER-STX-UNIT N731H - Ticket - Stage 8.pdf, p.1)
- **Wildcat Hollow S512HF — Stage 27**: estimated stage cost **$28,681.85** (WESTLAKE RESOURCES/WILDCAT HOLLOW-16-33-PGN S512HF/Completions/Frac/REPORT WILDCAT HOLLOW-16-33-PGN S512HF Stage 27.pdf, p.1)

**The Wrangler N731H stage was higher, by roughly $10,622.75** ($39,304.60 vs. $28,681.85).

Notes on scope:
- For each well, this is the only *parsed/readable* frac ticket carrying an "estimated stage cost" field in the corpus sample — Wrangler N731H's other frac tickets (Stage 69, Stage 21 plots) and Wildcat Hollow S512HF's other ticket (Stage 54) are unparsed vendor PDFs, so I could not pull their cost figures for comparison.
- Since these are different, arbitrarily-selected stages (Stage 8 vs. Stage 27) rather than matched stage numbers, this comparison reflects the specific stages for which readable cost data exists, not necessarily each well's overall average per-stage cost. If you want a fuller comparison, the remaining frac tickets could be parsed on demand, or a well-level "Completion Packet"/summary AFE (not found for either well in this sample) would give an average per-stage cost across the full job.

question: Which frac stage saw the higher maximum treating pressure: the Wrangler N734H stage or the Wildcat Hollow S512HF stage?

expected_citations.key: FORMENTERA SOUTH TEXAS/WRANGLER-STX-UNIT N734H/Completions/Frac/WRANGLER-STX-UNIT N734H - Print Out - Stage 37.pdf
WESTLAKE RESOURCES/WILDCAT HOLLOW-16-33-PGN S512HF/Completions/Frac/REPORT WILDCAT HOLLOW-16-33-PGN S512HF Stage 27.pdf

notes: Correct if it names Wrangler N734H as higher; both pressures (10,998 and 8,957 psi) should be supportable (±10 psi).
agent_answer:
Both wells have exactly one already-parsed (readable) frac stage report in the sample, and each contains a maximum treating pressure value:

- **Wrangler-STX-Unit N734H, Stage 37**: max treating pressure **10,998 psi** (FORMENTERA SOUTH TEXAS/WRANGLER-STX-UNIT N734H/Completions/Frac/WRANGLER-STX-UNIT N734H - Print Out - Stage 37.pdf, p.1)
- **Wildcat Hollow-16-33-PGN S512HF, Stage 27**: max treating pressure **8,957 psi** (WESTLAKE RESOURCES/WILDCAT HOLLOW-16-33-PGN S512HF/Completions/Frac/REPORT WILDCAT HOLLOW-16-33-PGN S512HF Stage 27.pdf, p.1)

question: What initial mud weight was used on the surface interval per the drilling-fluid recap for the Bull Mountain 31-18-DIV S611HF?

expected_citations.key: WESTLAKE RESOURCES/BULL MOUNTAIN-31-18-DIV S611HF/Drilling/Mud & Fluids/FORMENTERA -EOW RECAP - BULL MOUNTAIN 31-18-DIV S611HF - NABORS B-23.pdf

notes: Surface discussion: 'Started with 8.6 ppg MW.' Accept 8.6 ppg / lb/gal (±0.1).
agent_answer: Per the drilling-fluid recap for the Bull Mountain 31-18-DIV S611HF, the surface interval (0–1,655 ft MD, 13.5 in hole section, freshwater system) was **started with 8.6 ppg mud weight**, which was later maintained/increased to 8.7–8.9 ppg to help bring cuttings to surface ahead of running casing (WESTLAKE RESOURCES/BULL MOUNTAIN-31-18-DIV S611HF/Drilling/Mud & Fluids/FORMENTERA -EOW RECAP - BULL MOUNTAIN 31-18-DIV S611HF - NABORS B-23.pdf, page 3).

question: To what total depth (ftKB) is casing run on the Christine's Lucky Draw 6H per its casing summary?
notes: Casing summary depth reference: Surface to 9,716.0 ftKB (deepest string). Accept 9,716 ft (±5 ft).
agent_answer: The casing summary for **Christine's Lucky Draw 6H** shows casing run to a total depth of **9,716.0 ftKB** (surface to 9,716.0 ftKB), covering the conductor, surface, and production casing strings — (FP GRIFFIN/CHRISTINE'S LUCKY DRAW 6H/Drilling/Casing & Cement/422213189300-CHRISTINE_S_LUCKY_DRAW_6H-Casing_Reports-0002.pdf.PDF, page 1).
